title: Fluffy Fresh Syrian Chickpea Pilaf
description: A quick, fluffy, and sugar‑free Syrian chickpea pilaf that uses a sous‑vide technique to lock in moisture and flavor, finished with aromatic spices and a bright citrus note.
instructions: Rinse the soaked chickpeas and vacuum‑seal them in a bag with 2 cups of water, 1 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt.
Place the bag in a sous‑vide water bath set to 65°C (149°F) and cook for 2 hours.
After cooking, carefully remove the bag and drain the chickpeas, reserving the cooking liquid.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and turmeric to the onions and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the drained chickpeas and the reserved sous‑vide liquid, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and let the mixture cook for 10 minutes, allowing the chickpeas to absorb the spices and become fluffy.
Stir in the lemon zest and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
